title: larix archangelica
intro: Larix archangelica, also known as tansy, is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds that have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. Tansy has been used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of menstrual cramps, bloating, and indigestion, as well as to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of tansy can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and it is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
